macro_segments.title: Introduction and Previous Day's Context: Tumtum and Gender Categories

micro_segments.title: Review of tumtum case and gender binary discussion from previous day
Abaye's position that tumtum gets neither male nor female portion; subjective intent of giver
Digression on why father gives more to daughter; Tosafot's question on Avraham's daughter Bakol

macro_segments.title: The Core Problem: Can Property Be Transferred to an Unborn Fetus (Ubar)?

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna's position that hamezakeh le'ubar lo kanah—fetus cannot take possession
Gemara's attempt to reinterpret Mishna: perhaps transfer occurs when child is born, not while fetus
Rav Huna's objection even to 'liksheteiled' (when born) model—fetus cannot take possession at any point
Rav Nachman's compromise: no transfer now, but kinyan takes effect when child is born
Rav Sheshet's expansive position: mezakeh le'ubar kanah even while fetus; both now and when born work

macro_segments.title: Rav Sheshet's Source: The Ger Case (Non-Jew Who Converted Dies)

micro_segments.title: Story of ger who dies; Israelites loot his property; they learn he has a son or pregnant wife
They return the property; then hear the son died or wife miscarried
Question: Why couldn't they take possession the first time if no heir ultimately existed?
Abaye: fetus can inherit naturally; here it's irrelevant to mezakeh discussion
Rava: fetus doesn't inherit; possession was weak (rifyon) because heir might exist

macro_segments.title: Distinguishing Abaye and Rava: Does Uncertainty Affect Kinyan Subjectively or Objectively?

micro_segments.title: Nafka mina: Person hears child is dead, but child later dies; does subjective certainty matter?
Abaye's logic: if your subjective intent is sincere, kinyan works even if fetus didn't own it
Rava's logic: if fetus owns it objectively, your sincere but wrong belief doesn't help

macro_segments.title: Can Fetus Inherit? Mishna in Niddah and Rav Sheshet's Explanation

micro_segments.title: Mishna: child one day old can inherit and bequeath; but fetus cannot
Why does it matter? If child inherits from mother and passes to paternal brothers
Rav Sheshet: fetus could inherit from mother, but Mishna assumes mother dies before giving birth
Gemara: but fetus dies when mother dies in childbirth; Tosafot: not if violent death or late gestation

macro_segments.title: Alternative Explanation: Firstborn's Double Portion (Chelek Bechora)

micro_segments.title: Why tell us child inherits and bequeaths if it all goes to brothers anyway?
Answer: child's existence before father's death affects calculation of firstborn's double portion
Illustration: with three born sons, firstborn gets one full unit; with four brothers (including fetus), less
Rav bar Rav Yosef in Rava's name: only born children factor into firstborn's double portion calculation
Child born after father dies: not entitled to double portion; must be born before father's death

macro_segments.title: Halacha Ruling and Final Positions on Mezakeh Le'Ubar

micro_segments.title: Halacha: we rule like Rava's positions on inheritance and firstborn's portion
Rav Yitzchak in Rav Yochanan's name: hamezakeh le'ubar lo kanah—transfer to fetus does not work
Exception for own child: sincere paternal intent may allow transfer to one's own unborn child
Shmuel's attempt to publicize that mezakeh le'ubar kanah—overruled by halacha that it does not work

topical_tags.term: hamezakeh le'ubar lo kanah (cannot transfer ownership to fetus)
mezakeh le'ubar kanah (can transfer ownership to fetus)
rifyon (weakening of possession due to uncertainty)
